title: Define, Document, Approve, and Enforce Access Restrictions for Change
description: Define, document, approve, and enforce physical and logical access restrictions associated with changes to organizational systems.

checks.name: Only Authorized Users Can Install Software
checks.purpose: Verify standard users do not have local administrator rights that would allow unauthorized software installation or system changes

checks.name: Local Administrator Accounts are Controlled
checks.purpose: Confirm the local Administrators group contains only authorized accounts and the built-in Administrator account is disabled or renamed

checks.name: UAC is Enabled and Configured
checks.purpose: Verify User Account Control (UAC) is enabled and set to prompt for credentials on the secure desktop

checks.name: Only Authorized Users Can Install Software or Modify Roles
checks.purpose: Verify only authorized administrators can install software or add/remove server roles and features

checks.name: Local Administrator Accounts are Controlled
checks.purpose: Confirm the local Administrators group contains only authorized accounts and the built-in Administrator account is disabled or renamed

checks.name: UAC is Enabled and Configured
checks.purpose: Verify User Account Control is enabled and configured appropriately for server context

checks.name: Change-Related Privileged Access is Logged
checks.purpose: Confirm privileged access used to make system changes is captured in the audit log with user identity and timestamp

checks.name: Only Root and Authorized Sudoers Can Modify System Configuration
checks.purpose: Verify direct root login is disabled and sudo access is restricted to named authorized users or groups via /etc/sudoers

checks.name: Package Installation Requires Elevated Privileges
checks.purpose: Confirm standard users cannot install packages by verifying they lack sudo rights to apt, yum, dnf, or rpm

checks.name: Sudoers File is Restricted and Audited
checks.purpose: Verify /etc/sudoers and /etc/sudoers.d/ contain only authorized entries and are watched by auditd for unauthorized modification

checks.name: Only Root and Authorized Sudoers Can Modify System Configuration
checks.purpose: Verify direct root login is disabled and sudo access is restricted to named authorized users or groups

checks.name: Package Installation Requires Elevated Privileges
checks.purpose: Confirm standard users cannot install packages by verifying they lack sudo rights to apt or dpkg

checks.name: Sudoers File is Restricted and Audited
checks.purpose: Verify /etc/sudoers and /etc/sudoers.d/ contain only authorized entries and are watched by auditd for unauthorized modification

remediation: Remove standard users from the local Administrators group. Enable and configure UAC. Disable or rename the built-in Administrator account. On Linux, disable direct root login and restrict sudo to named authorized users. Watch sudoers with auditd. Ensure all privileged access used for system changes is logged.
